commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair lane is needed; maintainers should review the narrow patch and run focused validation before deciding whether to merge.; Cleared: The diff only changes in-process plugin-loader memoization and cache invalidation, with no dependency, workflow, secret, package-resolution, or external code-execution change. (sha=3f25ea6433451d008054c6b86bb95f0ba4e96add)
commands.review_summary:
Memoizes the plugin install-records fallback read in `src/plugins/loader.ts` and resets that memo when plugin loader or registry caches are invalidated.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows the fallback sync install-records read happens before cache reuse when `options.installRecords` is absent, and the PR discussion includes live before/after timing evidence; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair lane is needed; maintainers should review the narrow patch and run focused validation before deciding whether to merge.

target.title: fix(plugins): memoize loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecordsSync on hot path
target.body:
## Summary

- Memoize `loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecordsSync` result in `resolvePluginLoadCacheContext` so the sync file read does not fire on every `resolvePluginTools()` call, including LRU cache hits.
- Invalidate the memoized install records alongside the plugin loader cache in `clearPluginLoaderCache`.

Install records do not change at runtime, so the synchronous file read was redundant after the first call. This was causing unnecessary I/O on every plugin tool resolution, compounding under concurrent embedded runs.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `resolvePluginLoadCacheContext` calls `loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecordsSync` (a synchronous file read) on every `resolvePluginTools()` invocation, including LRU cache hits. Since install records do not change at runtime, the repeated I/O is redundant and compounds under concurrent embedded runs (#77348).

**Real environment tested:** Node.js v22.22.0 on Linux (same runtime as OpenClaw production). Ran a benchmark script that simulates 100 consecutive `resolvePluginTools()` calls with a 1.6 KB install records file (50 plugin entries), measuring file read count and elapsed time before and after the memoization patch.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```bash
node memoize_benchmark.mjs
```
The script creates a mock install records JSON file, then runs 100 iterations of the original (un-memoized) read and 100 iterations of the memoized version, counting `readFileSync` calls and measuring wall-clock time.

**Evidence after fix:**
```
=== Memoize Benchmark: loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecordsSync ===
Iterations: 100
Install records file size: 1.6 KB

WITHOUT memoization (original behavior):
  File reads: 100
  Total time: 4.95 ms

WITH memoization (this PR):
  File reads: 1
  Total time: 0.12 ms

Reduction:
  File reads eliminated: 99 (99%)
  Time saved: 4.83 ms (98%)
```

Independent community benchmark by @holgergruenhagen on a real OpenClaw 2026.5.7 setup (Linux VPS, Discord-driven) confirmed ~30% end-to-end latency reduction: MessageCreate latency dropped from 13.30s to 7.60s, and `loadOpenClawPlugins` calls dropped from 12× (4 MISS + 8 HIT) to 2× (0 MISS + 2 HIT). See [#77379 (comment)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77379#issuecomment-4432687240).

**Observed result after the fix:** After applying the memoization patch, `loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecordsSync` is called only once instead of on every `resolvePluginTools()` call. The 100-iteration benchmark shows a 99% reduction in file reads (100 → 1) and a 98% reduction in wall-clock time (4.95 ms → 0.12 ms). Cache invalidation in `clearPluginLoaderCache` and `clearPluginRegistryLoadCache` correctly resets the memo, allowing a fresh read on the next call.

**What was not tested:** Live OpenClaw gateway with actual plugin lifecycle (load, hot-reload, registry update). The benchmark validates the I/O reduction of the memoization pattern in isolation.

Closes #77348
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
